zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Alice Boleyn於大約1420年出生在英國肯特紐登時，她的父親Sir Geoffrey Boleyn I41歲，母親Alice Bracton36歲。她於1448年之前與Henri Aucher在英國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1485年在英國諾福爾可艾爾善姆享年67歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可布利可林。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Alice Boleyn于大约1420年出生在英国肯特紐登时，她的父亲Sir Geoffrey Boleyn I41岁，母亲Alice Bracton36岁。她于1448年之前与Henri Aucher在英国结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于大约1485年在英国諾福爾可艾爾善姆享年67岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾福爾可布利可林。

nameMeanings.name: Boleyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Bullen  .
Flemish: derivative of the personal name Bol, from Baldwin .

variants.name: Bullen
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from the French Channel port of Boulogne, recorded in Latin sources both as Gessoriacum and as Bononia. The latter name is clearly the source of the modern placename. It is ostensibly a derivative of Latin bonus ‘good’ (compare Bolognese ), but may in fact come from a Gaulish element bona ‘foundation’. Boulogne has long been a major trading port between England and France.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Alice Boleyn was born about 1420, in Newenden, Kent, England, her father, Sir Geoffrey Boleyn I, was 41 and her mother, Alice Bracton, was 36. She married Henri Aucher before 1448, in England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died about 1485, in Aylsham, Norfolk, England, at the age of 67, and was buried in Blickling, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Alice Boleyn est née environ 1420, à Newenden, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Sir Geoffrey Boleyn I, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Alice Bracton, avait 36 ans. Elle a épousé Henri Aucher avant 1448, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1485, à Aylsham, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterrée à Blickling, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel Alice Boleyn um 1420 in Newenden, Kent, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Geoffrey Boleyn I 41 und ihre Mutter Alice Bracton 36. Sie hat vor 1448 in England Henri Aucher geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb um 1485 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Aylsham, Norfolk, England und wurde in Blickling, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Alice Boleyn è nata il circa 1420 nel Newenden, Kent, England, sua padre, Sir Geoffrey Boleyn I, aveva 41 anni e sua madre, Alice Bracton, aveva 36 anni. Lei sposato Henri Aucher prima del 1448, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì circa 1485, nel Aylsham, Norfolk, England, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolta a Blickling, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Alice Boleynが約1420年にイギリスケントニューエンデンで生まれた時，父Sir Geoffrey Boleyn Iは41歳，母Alice Bractonは36歳であった。1448年以前にイギリスでHenri Aucherと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1485年にイギリスノーフォルクエイルシャムで67歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーフォルクブリックリングにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Alice Boleyn(이)가 대략 1420년 영국 켄트 뉴든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Geoffrey Boleyn I(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Bracton(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1448년 이전 영국에서 Henri Aucher(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1485년 영국 노르폴크 아이샤에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 블릭링에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Alice Boleyn nasceu aproximadamente 1420, em Newenden, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Geoffrey Boleyn I, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Alice Bracton, tinha 36 anos. Ela casou-se com Henri Aucher antes 1448, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1485, em Aylsham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 67 anos, e foi sepultada em Blickling, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Alice Boleyn родилась около 1420 г. в местности нюенден, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Geoffrey Boleyn I было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Alice Bracton, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henri Aucher перед 1448 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла около 1485 г. в местности аилшам, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 67 лет и была похоронена в местности бликклинг, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel Alice Boleyn nació aproximadamente 1420, en Newenden, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Geoffrey Boleyn I, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Alice Bracton, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Henri Aucher antes de 1448, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió aproximadamente 1485, en Aylsham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultada en Blickling, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
